commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cd68e0e42c36f610d5deaf22dd1873b704d4c7c2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds one Markdown RFC proposing optional experimental Iroh Gateway transport support with Iroh config, pairing, pinning, key handling, and security-audit expectations.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a design RFC for a new optional Gateway transport, not a current behavior bug. The concrete check is source and GitHub PR state: current main lacks the Iroh RFC while the open queue already has multiple 0009 proposals.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The immediate blocker is RFC queue ownership: a maintainer should assign the number before any automated repair renames this proposal.

target.title: RFC 0009: Iroh Gateway transport
target.body:
## Summary

Adds Iroh as an optional, experimental OpenClaw Gateway transport so clients can pair and reconnect without requiring a VPN such as Tailscale.

## Review focus

- Whether the first implementation should use native Gateway protocol framing over Iroh QUIC streams or an Iroh-to-localhost bridge.
- Exact Iroh fields for the setup-code payload.
- Whether paired client records should store and enforce client Iroh `EndpointId`s.
- Which Iroh endpoint configuration fields should be exposed 1:1 under `gateway.iroh.endpoint`.
- Whether browser/WASM support should be required for the first experiment or left as future work.

## RFC lifecycle

- Status: draft
- Maintainer discussion thread: https://discord.com/channels/1456350064065904867/1517996004778180780
- Implementation issue: created only if accepted
target.author: bjesuiter

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
